Comment: Cross-Account Refresh Orchestrator - Full 5-phase flow with database, EFS, and EKS operations

ValidateInputs.Comment: Validate all inputs and required resources before starting refresh

CheckValidationStatus.Comment: If validation failed, route to failure notification. Otherwise check DryRun mode.

CheckDryRunMode.Comment: If DryRun=true, return validation report without executing refresh

DryRunComplete.Comment: Return validation report for dry-run mode

PrepareRefresh.Comment: Prepare resource names and retrieve current tags from destination account

CheckS3Present.Comment: Normalise optional S3 block before MergePrepareResults forwards it. Legacy inputs carry no S3 key; injecting {Enabled:false} when absent lets MergePrepareResults use a hard $.S3 reference without a States.Runtime error, preserving strict ORCH-05 no-op behaviour.

CheckEFSReplicationMode.Comment: When EFS cross-account replication is active, use synchronized cutoff flow to ensure DB snapshot and EFS data are from the same point in time. Otherwise use standard parallel flow.

Phase1WithReplication.Comment: Phase 1 (replication mode): Run mysqldump and EFS replication+sync in parallel. DB restore is deferred to SynchronizedCutoffAndRestore to ensure temporal consistency between DB snapshot and EFS data.

Branches.Comment: Branch A: MySQLDump on old cluster (same as standard path)

RunMysqlDump_Repl.Comment: Dump tables from old cluster to EFS before restore

Branches.Comment: Branch B: EFS cross-account replication setup, wait for sync, create mount targets, and optionally store SSM

SetupCrossAccountReplication_Repl.Comment: Setup EFS replication from source to destination account (supports cross-region via ProxyLambdaArn and existing destination EFS via CreateDestinationEFS/DestinationFileSystemId)

CheckReplicationSync_Repl.Comment: Wait for EFS replication to reach near-realtime sync before cutoff

PrepareEfsResult_Repl.Comment: Format replication result to match standard EFS output structure

StoreSubpathInSSM_Repl.Comment: Replication mode: media is at EFS root, so store subpath '.' directly (SubpathMode=root) WITHOUT running the aws-backup-restore_* detector lambda, which would wrongly pick up a stale aws-backup-restore_* dir left on the EFS.

SynchronizedCutoffAndRestore.Comment: Synchronized cutoff: delete EFS replication and create manual DB snapshot simultaneously, then restore DB from that snapshot. Both operations start at the same instant T, ensuring DB and EFS data are temporally consistent.

Branches.Comment: Branch A: Delete EFS replication to freeze EFS data at time T

DeleteEFSReplication_Sync.Comment: Delete replication to make destination EFS writable and freeze data at time T (supports cross-region via destination-side deletion)

ReplicationDeleteFailed_Sync.Cause: Failed to delete EFS replication during synchronized cutoff

Branches.Comment: Branch B: Clean up leftover restore cluster, then restore DB from a fresh manual snapshot created at time T

EnsureRestoreClusterNotExists_Sync.Comment: Clean up any leftover -restore cluster from failed refresh

RestoreDatabase_Sync.Comment: Restore database from a fresh manual snapshot - snapshot is created NOW at time T, synchronized with EFS replication cutoff (supports cross-region via ProxyLambdaArn)

DatabaseRefreshFailed_Sync.Cause: Database restore process failed during synchronized cutoff

MergeResultsFromReplication.Comment: Normalize results from replication path to match standard Phase1Results format: [0]=mysqldump, [1]=DB, [2]=EFS. This allows downstream states to use the same JSONPath references regardless of which path was taken.

Phase1DataRefresh.Comment: Phase 1: Restore database and EFS in parallel, optionally run mysqldump on old cluster

Branches.Comment: Branch A: MySQLDump on old cluster (optional, runs in parallel with restore)

RunMysqlDump.Comment: Dump tables from old cluster to EFS before restore completes

CheckDatabaseEnabled_Restore.Comment: Skip DB restore if Database.Enabled is false

EnsureRestoreClusterNotExists.Comment: Clean up any leftover -restore cluster from failed refresh

RestoreDatabase.Comment: Restore database from source snapshot to destination account

Branches.Comment: Branch C: EFS Restore chain with optional SSM subpath storage

CheckEFSCrossAccount.Comment: Check if EFS is cross-account by comparing Source.Account.AccountId with DestinationAccount.AccountId

SetupCrossAccountReplication.Comment: Cross-account: Setup EFS replication from source to destination

CheckReplicationSyncInitial.Comment: Cross-account: Wait for initial replication to complete

PrepareEfsResultFromReplication.Comment: Format replication result to match RestoreEFS output structure

RestoreEFS.Comment: Restore EFS from AWS Backup. JSONata so cross-account fields (CrossAccount/DestinationVaultArn/CopyRoleArn) and the optional explicit RecoveryPointArn/SourceVaultName are passed only when present in EFS.Backup (JSONata omits absent keys) — same-account restore is unaffected. Reused by both local and cross-account backup_restore strategies; converges to CreateEFSMountTargets like replication.

StoreSubpathInSSM.Comment: Find EFS subpath and store both EFS ID and subpath in SSM

Branches.Comment: Branch D: Optional S3 cross-account replication (setup + optional backfill). Self-guarded by CheckS3Enabled: a run without an S3 block, or with S3.Enabled=false, reduces to an instant no-op Succeed (ORCH-05/D-03), leaving the reachable-set strictly identical to before. No teardown (D-06).

CheckS3Enabled.Comment: Activation guard (D-03/ORCH-05): only the operational path requires $.S3.Enabled present AND true. Absent S3 key fails IsPresent and falls to the no-op Succeed Default.

CheckS3SetupNeeded.Comment: Copy mode is a one-shot seed via S3PutObjectCopy and does NOT use (or leave) a live ReplicationConfiguration on the source, so it skips setup entirely. Replicate mode needs the live config, so it runs setup first.

S3SetupReplication.Comment: Always invoked when S3.Enabled (D-04). Reshapes the EFS-mirror S3 input block to the frozen setup_cross_account_replication contract (D-10). No Credentials here: the cross-account assume-role happens inside the S3 SFN.

CheckS3BackfillEnabled.Comment: Backfill sub-toggle (D-05): run_batch + check_batch only when $.S3.Backfill.Enabled present AND true; otherwise skip straight to the Succeed.

CheckS3BackfillMode.Comment: Backfill mode toggle. Mode=copy -> S3PutObjectCopy job (copies EVERY object incl. replicas that S3ReplicateObject skips; needed to seed from a bucket populated by inbound replication). Absent/anything else -> replicate mode (S3ReplicateObject, incremental via NONE/FAILED, reuses the live config).

S3RunBatchCopy.Comment: Dispatch the S3 Batch Copy job (copy mode). Copies the full current contents of the source bucket to the single target Destinations[0] via S3PutObjectCopy, including replica objects. Same output envelope as run_batch_replication so S3PrepareJobId is shared. Copy targets one bucket per job; run per destination.

S3RunBatch.Comment: Dispatch the S3 Batch Replication job (D-05). Reshape to the frozen run_batch_replication contract. No manifest/override field is passed: the NONE/FAILED filter stays inside the frozen SFN (D-08). ReportBucketArn is intentionally omitted (the contract treats absence via ShouldEnableReport).

S3PrepareJobId.Comment: Re-extract the .sync:2 envelope (D-10): run_batch emits $.S3BatchResult.Output.JobId; check_batch expects $.BatchJob.JobId. Produce $.S3BatchJob.JobId for the next Task.

S3CheckBatch.Comment: Poll the batch job to completion (D-05/D-07). Reshape to the frozen check_batch_replication contract: BatchJob.JobId from the re-extracted $.S3BatchJob.JobId, plus SourceAccount.

S3ReplicationFailed.Cause: S3 cross-account replication setup or backfill failed

CheckRunSqlScriptsOption.Comment: Phase 1 post-process: optionally run SQL scripts on restored cluster

RunSqlScripts.Comment: Execute SQL scripts from S3 on the restored cluster via run-sql Lambda

CheckMysqlImportOption.Comment: Phase 1 post-process: optionally import mysqldump data into restored cluster

RunMysqlImport.Comment: Import mysqldump data into restored cluster via EKS job

Phase2PreSwitchEKS.Comment: Phase 2: Scale down EKS services and delete storage before cluster switch

CheckEKSEnabled_PreSwitch.Comment: Check if ScaleServices is enabled for pre-switch scale down

CheckManageStoragePreSwitch.Comment: Check if ManageStorage is enabled for pre-switch storage deletion

ScaleDownServices.Comment: Scale down all EKS services to 0 replicas before storage switch

DeleteEKSStorage.Comment: Delete PVC/PV/StorageClass for old EFS before cluster switch

CheckEFSCrossAccountFinalSync.Comment: Check if EFS cross-account replication needs final sync and deletion before switch. Skip if replication was already deleted during synchronized cutoff (replication path).

CheckReplicationSyncFinal.Comment: Cross-account: Final sync check with flag file verification before deleting replication

DeleteEFSReplication.Comment: Cross-account: Delete replication to make destination EFS writable

ExecuteClusterSwitchSequence.Comment: Phase 3: Execute cluster switch via ClusterSwitchSequence sub-SFN (rename old, tag, rename new)

CheckRotateSecretsOption.Comment: Check for unified Database.Secrets.Sync (new) or legacy Database.Options.RotateSecrets

SyncDatabaseSecrets.Comment: Sync DB user secrets from source to destination (password + endpoints). Optionally rotate via Lambda.

CheckConfigSyncOption.Comment: Optionally sync config items (secrets/parameters) to destination account

DescribeSourceClusterForSync.Comment: Get source cluster endpoints (writer + reader) for host replacement transforms

DescribeDestClusterForSync.Comment: Get destination (restored) cluster endpoints for host replacement transforms

InjectHostTransforms.Comment: Inject dynamic host replace transforms into ConfigSync items that have Type=SecretsManager and contain database paths. Replaces full writer and reader hostnames from source to destination.

ExecuteSyncConfigItems.Comment: Sync secrets and parameters from source to destination account

Phase4PostSwitchEKS.Comment: Phase 4: Create new EKS storage, scale up services, verify and restart. Resolve the EFS id created this run (if any) so storage creation can use it directly for a disposable EFS that has no /infra/efs/<name>/id SSM parameter; null falls back to the SSM lookup.

CheckEKSEnabled_PostSwitch.Comment: Check if ManageStorage is enabled for post-switch storage creation

CheckScaleServicesPostSwitch.Comment: Check if ScaleServices is enabled for post-switch scale up

CreateEKSStorage.Comment: Create PVC/PV/StorageClass for new EFS (reads EFS ID from SSM)

ScaleUpServices.Comment: Scale up EKS services to 1 replica with optional secret cleanup

VerifyAndRestartServices.Comment: Verify EFS subpath on services and restart if mismatch

RunArchiveJob.Comment: Sync MySQL dumps and media files to S3

CheckDeleteTargetAfterArchive.Comment: Disposable image-build flow: tear down the just-refreshed target cluster + EFS. Opt-in, default off.

DeleteTargetResources.Comment: Delete the disposable target DB cluster and target EFS created for this build.

TargetClusterDeleteDone.Comment: Emit a small summary (not the full accumulated state) so the Parallel aggregate stays under the 256KB limit.

ResolveTargetEfsId.Comment: Resolve the new/target EFS id using the same precedence as DeleteEFSReplication.

TargetEfsDeleteDone.Comment: Emit a small summary (not the full accumulated state) so the Parallel aggregate stays under the 256KB limit.

Phase5Cleanup.Comment: Phase 5: Cleanup old resources (delete/stop old cluster, delete old EFS, scale down nodegroup)

CheckNotifyEnabled_Success.Comment: Skip notification if Notifications.Enabled is false or absent

CheckNotifyEnabled_Failure.Comment: Skip notification if Notifications.Enabled is false or absent

ConfigLoadFailed.Cause: Failed to load configuration from S3

RefreshFailed.Cause: The refresh process encountered an error

BuildRefreshSummary.Comment: Collapse the accumulated orchestration state into a compact execution output: resource names/ids + per-step success flags. Detailed per-step timings come from the execution history (see scripts/refresh_report.py), not from this state.
